text: The surgeon inserts an optical trocar using the modified Scandinavian technique in the Inter-Anglican crease and places a 5mm working port in the suprapubic region. Using a gut-friendly grasp, the surgeon follows the tinea coli to locate the appendix, which is observed lying retrocecally.
The surgeon identifies the ureter as a white structure running along the pelvic side wall, with the ovarian vessels located above it. The appendix is visibly acutely inflamed.

The surgeon inserts a 5mm working port in the left mid-clavicular line, positioned between the optical port and the suprapubic port. Using a ligature Maryland in the left hand and a grasping instrument in the right hand, the surgeon begins dissecting the mesoappendix along the long axis of the appendix, ensuring no fatty tissue remains attached to facilitate removal through the port.
The surgeon prepares to ligate the base of the appendix using a LigaClip, having replaced the suprapubic working port with a Canon. The appendix is positioned for ligation.
